summary: Added automated LQA + LLM-second-pass as an honest intermediate quality gate between LLM-drafted (needs-layout-check) and native-reviewed. Tools/localization-lqa.py runs format-specifier parity, empty-value, identical-to-English-outside-keep, length-outlier, and mojibake-suspect checks across all 18 per-locale review return files. Each entry receives an `lqa` block: status=passed (6354 total) / warning (54) / reverted (0). The format-specifier check was tuned to treat missing %d in stringsdict plural_category=one as idiomatic (Arabic-style 'one item' drops %d intentionally) rather than auto-reverting. Tools/localization-review-status.py was extended to surface LQA counts per locale in both stdout table and STATUS.md markdown. localization/review/LQA.md is the aggregate report. docs/workflows/localization-translation-quality.md records the gate explicitly as 'NOT a native-review claim'. No app resources were modified. No provenance.native_reviewed flags were flipped. app-localization-native-review-intake remains blocked.
missing_proof_levels: build-tested (for translated content quality, not just compilation)

contract_status_changes.contract: Automated LQA gate between LLM-drafted and native-reviewed
contract_status_changes.before: Per-locale return files carried only review_status=needs-layout-check or keep-english-term, with no machine-verified consistency check. There was no honest intermediate signal between 'LLM produced a draft' and 'a native reviewer accepted it'.
contract_status_changes.after: Each entry in every per-locale return file carries an lqa block with status=passed | warning | reverted plus the issues list. The dashboard counts these. The LQA gate is documented as 'NOT a native-review claim' in localization/review/LQA.md, localization/review/STATUS.md, the tool docstrings, and docs/workflows/localization-translation-quality.md. The intake slice remains blocked until human native review supplies corrected values.

residual_risks: The LQA only checks deterministic surface properties. It does not catch wrong gender, wrong formality, wrong idiom, or wrong argument order — those need a native reviewer.

The format-specifier check skips strict equality for stringsdict plural_category=one so Arabic-style 'one item' translations pass. This is intentional but could let through a real bug in that specific category if the LLM-draft dropped %d unintentionally.

Length-outlier warnings are noisy for CJK (zh-Hans 21 warnings, zh-Hant 16) because CJK legitimately compresses text. The threshold is relaxed to 0.25x for CJK locales, but UI layout under translated short labels still wants screenshot verification.

Auto-revert (status=reverted) drops a candidate translation back to English and re-tags it needs-translation. Currently 0 entries triggered this. If a future LLM pass introduces a bad specifier, the auto-revert will silently swap it; the lqa.auto_fix_action field records the change but downstream tooling that ignores this field will misread the resource state.
lqa.status=passed could mistakenly be read as a quality claim. The dashboard, report, and docs all explicitly say it is not, but downstream agents must be careful.
The LQA does not run on app resources directly — it operates on per-locale return files. A future native-reviewed locale that bypasses the return-file workflow would not gain LQA coverage.
recommended_next_reason: Clean stop. app-localization-native-review-intake remains blocked on human-supplied native review of at least one locale. The automated LQA does not unblock it. No new actionable slice is created from this work.
